courses.title: Introduction to Kabbalah (Online - Evening)
courses.description: A gentle beginner-friendly introduction to the core concepts of Kabbalah, exploring sefirot, spiritual practice, and personal transformation in a supportive online setting.
courses.syllabus:
Week 1: What is Kabbalah?
Week 2: The Tree of Life and the sefirot
Week 3: Soul journeys and spiritual practice
Week 4: Prayer, meditation, and intention (kavannah)
Week 5: Kabbalah and everyday life
Week 6: Integration and closing ritual.
courses.schedule_summary: Mondays, 7:00–8:30 pm (online via Zoom) for 6 weeks.

courses.title: Introduction to Kabbalah (In-Person - Evening)
courses.description: An in-person exploration of foundational Kabbalistic teachings, with text study, meditation, and group discussion.
courses.syllabus:
Week 1: Origins of Jewish mysticism
Week 2: The inner life of mitzvot
Week 3: The Tree of Life
Week 4: Kabbalah and prayer
Week 5: Light and vessels
Week 6: Personal spiritual practice
Week 7: Sacred community
Week 8: Closing ritual.
courses.schedule_summary: Thursdays, 7:30–9:00 pm in person at our center for 8 weeks.

courses.title: Introduction to Kabbalah Intensive (Hybrid)
courses.description: A 4-week intensive introduction to Kabbalah combining in-person gatherings with livestream access for remote participants.
courses.syllabus:
Week 1: Core images and maps of Kabbalah
Week 2: The four worlds and levels of soul
Week 3: Working with divine names and intention
Week 4: Designing your own mystical practice.
courses.schedule_summary: Tuesdays, 7:30–9:30 pm (in person + online) for 4 weeks.

courses.title: Introduction to Kabbalah (Weekend Mornings)
courses.description: A slower-paced introduction to Kabbalah meeting on Sunday mornings, ideal for those who prefer daytime learning.
courses.syllabus: Weeks 1–6: Core mystical ideas, texts, and spiritual exercises, with additional optional readings.

courses.title: Jewish Mindfulness Meditation Series
courses.description: Learn core mindfulness practices through a Jewish lens, integrating breath, body awareness, and traditional texts.
courses.syllabus:
Week 1: Arriving in the present moment
Week 2: Compassion and chesed
Week 3: Working with difficult emotions
Week 4: Integrating practice into daily life.

courses.title: Hebrew Reading Crash Course
courses.description: A practical, supportive course to help you learn to decode Hebrew letters and vowels for prayer and study.
courses.syllabus: Sound-letter correspondence, basic syllables, reading common blessings, and building confidence.
courses.schedule_summary: Tuesdays, 7:30–9:00 pm in person for 5 weeks.

courses.title: Exploring the Weekly Torah Portion
courses.description: A rolling class that dives into the weekly parashah with classical and contemporary commentaries.
courses.syllabus: Each week focuses on the upcoming Torah portion with group discussion and practical takeaways.

courses.title: Grief and Healing Through Jewish Tradition
courses.description: A supportive course for those in mourning, exploring Jewish texts, rituals, and practices for navigating grief.
courses.syllabus: Shiva and shloshim, saying Kaddish, Psalms of comfort, memory practices, and creating personal ritual.

courses.title: Shabbat Cooking Workshop
courses.description: Hands-on cooking sessions featuring traditional and modern Shabbat recipes, with teachings on the meaning behind the foods.
courses.syllabus:
Week 1: Challah and blessings
Week 2: Main dishes and sides
Week 3: Desserts and Havdalah treats.
courses.schedule_summary: Sundays, 1:00–3:00 pm in our kitchen for 3 weeks.

courses.title: Advanced Kabbalistic Texts
courses.description: A deep dive into primary Kabbalistic sources for students with prior experience in Jewish text study.
courses.syllabus: Selections from Sefer Yetzirah, Zohar, and Lurianic writings, in Hebrew/Aramaic with commentary.
courses.schedule_summary: Wednesdays, 8:00–9:45 pm in person for 12 weeks.

courses.title: Parenting with Jewish Values
courses.description: Explore how Jewish wisdom can support compassionate and grounded parenting.
courses.syllabus: Topics include Shalom Bayit (peace in the home), discipline and dignity, blessing our children, and creating family ritual.

courses.title: Hebrew Calligraphy and Ketubah Art
courses.description: An art-focused workshop exploring Hebrew calligraphy techniques and the tradition of ketubah (marriage contract) art.
courses.syllabus: Basic letter forms, layout and design, and creating a small illuminated text.
courses.schedule_summary: Tuesdays, 2:00–4:00 pm in the art studio for 4 weeks.

funds.name: Community Counseling Fund
funds.description: Supports subsidized spiritual counseling, grief support, and pastoral care for individuals and families in need.

funds.name: General Fund
funds.description: Sustains our core programs, staff, and communal gatherings throughout the year.

funds.name: Education Fund
funds.description: Provides scholarships and materials for classes, workshops, and youth learning.

newsletter_topics.name: Weekly Torah Portion
newsletter_topics.description: Insights, commentaries, and questions for reflection on the weekly parashah.

newsletter_topics.name: Mindfulness Practices
newsletter_topics.description: Short guided practices and teachings to support a mindful Jewish life.

newsletter_topics.name: Upcoming Ritual Workshops
newsletter_topics.description: Be the first to hear about upcoming ritual skills workshops and holiday prep sessions.

newsletter_topics.name: Community News & Updates
newsletter_topics.description: General announcements, event highlights, and organizational news.

ritual_leaders.name: Rabbi Miriam Gold
ritual_leaders.title: Senior Rabbi & Pastoral Counselor
ritual_leaders.bio: Rabbi Miriam Gold has over 15 years of experience guiding individuals and families through life-cycle rituals, grief, and spiritual growth. She is known for her warmth, clear teaching, and inclusive approach.

ritual_leaders.name: Rabbi David Stein
ritual_leaders.title: Ritual Director
ritual_leaders.bio: Rabbi Stein specializes in home-based rituals including Shabbat candle-lighting guidance, baby namings, and shiva minyanim. He brings a calm presence and clear step-by-step explanations.

ritual_leaders.name: Rabbi Yael Levy
ritual_leaders.title: Spiritual Leader
ritual_leaders.bio: Rabbi Yael focuses on prayer leadership, healing services, and helping families personalize rituals such as baby namings and Shabbat blessings.

ritual_leaders.name: Rabbi Aron Katz
ritual_leaders.title: Lifecycle & Education Rabbi
ritual_leaders.bio: Rabbi Aron guides families through baby namings, bnei mitzvah, and weddings, with an emphasis on education and family involvement.

ritual_leaders.name: Rabbi Rina Levinson
ritual_leaders.title: Shiva & Mourning Support Rabbi
ritual_leaders.bio: Rabbi Rina is dedicated to accompanying mourners, leading shiva minyanim, and offering compassionate spiritual counseling during times of loss.

ritual_leaders.name: Cantor Eli Mizrahi
ritual_leaders.title: Cantor & Ritual Leader
ritual_leaders.bio: Cantor Eli brings soulful music and strong leadership to Shabbat, holiday, and shiva services, with experience in both traditional and contemporary liturgy.

ritual_leaders.name: Rabbi Sarah Bloom
ritual_leaders.title: Family Rituals Coordinator
ritual_leaders.bio: Rabbi Sarah works closely with young families to create meaningful Shabbat practices at home and to plan baby namings and other life-cycle events.

ritual_leaders.name: Rabbi Josh Kaplan
ritual_leaders.title: Community Rabbi
ritual_leaders.bio: Rabbi Josh supports community members through all stages of life, with particular experience in shiva leadership and adult education.

ritual_leaders.name: Rabbi Tamar Rosen
ritual_leaders.title: Visiting Ritual Leader
ritual_leaders.bio: Rabbi Tamar travels throughout the city to lead home-based Shabbat rituals, havdalah, and holiday gatherings.

ritual_leaders.name: Rabbi Noam Reich
ritual_leaders.title: Online Ritual Facilitator
ritual_leaders.bio: Rabbi Noam specializes in online baby namings, shiva minyanim, and study sessions for families and communities at a distance.

ritual_leaders.name: Rabbi Levi Mandel
ritual_leaders.title: Senior Lifecycle Rabbi
ritual_leaders.bio: With decades of experience, Rabbi Levi brings steadiness and clarity to weddings, baby namings, unveilings, and shiva services.

ritual_leaders.name: Morah Hannah Green
ritual_leaders.title: Ritual Educator
ritual_leaders.bio: Hannah focuses on teaching the meaning behind rituals, offering Shabbat candle-lighting guidance and home ritual consultations.

ritual_services.name: Shabbat Candle-Lighting Guidance
ritual_services.description: Personalized instruction and support for setting up and leading Shabbat candle-lighting at home, including blessings, timing, and creating a peaceful ritual space.

ritual_services.name: Baby Naming Ceremony
ritual_services.description: A joyful ceremony to welcome a new baby into the covenant of the Jewish people, crafted together with your family and held at our synagogue, center, or in your home.

ritual_services.name: Book Shiva Minyan Leader
ritual_services.description: Arrange for an experienced rabbi or leader to guide a shiva minyan in your home, at our center, or online, including prayers, teachings, and space for sharing memories.

ritual_services.name: Healing Prayer Circle
ritual_services.description: A small-group gathering focused on prayers for healing, gentle song, and guided reflection for those facing illness or supporting loved ones.

ritual_services.name: Holiday Home Ritual Consultation
ritual_services.description: Work with a ritual leader to design meaningful home practices for upcoming holidays, tailored to your household.

store_categories.name: Shabbat & Home Rituals
store_categories.description: Candles, kiddush cups, challah covers, and other items to enrich Shabbat and daily home ritual.

store_categories.name: Learning Resources
store_categories.description: Books, study guides, and materials for Jewish learning and spiritual growth.

store_categories.name: Gifts
store_categories.description: Meaningful Judaica and gift items for celebrations and milestones.

support_groups.title: Online Beginner Grief Support Circle
support_groups.description: A gentle online circle for those new to grief support groups, offering check-ins, guided reflection, and Jewish wisdom for navigating loss.
support_groups.schedule_summary: Mondays this month, 7:30–8:45 pm (online via Zoom).

support_groups.title: Grief Support Group – Open to Newcomers
support_groups.description: An ongoing online group for adults who are grieving a recent or past loss, with space for sharing and supportive ritual.

support_groups.title: Morning Grief Support Circle
support_groups.description: An in-person morning group for those who prefer daytime meetings, including quiet reflection and light text study.

support_groups.title: Weekly Mindfulness Practice Group
support_groups.description: A weekly group focused on short guided meditations, mindful text study, and check-ins.

support_groups.title: Evening Mindfulness & Compassion Circle
support_groups.description: A small group that meets online in the evening to practice mindfulness and cultivate compassion using Jewish sources.

support_groups.title: Parenting Support Circle
support_groups.description: Connect with other parents to share challenges and draw on Jewish wisdom for raising children with resilience and kindness.
support_groups.schedule_summary: Tuesdays, 7:15–8:30 pm (in person with Zoom option).

support_groups.title: Caregivers Support Group
support_groups.description: A supportive space for those caring for aging parents, partners, or loved ones living with illness.

support_groups.title: Teen Grief Support Group
support_groups.description: A closed, time-limited group for teens processing the loss of a loved one, incorporating art, writing, and ritual.
support_groups.schedule_summary: Sundays, 3:00–4:30 pm at our center (8-week cycle).

product_subcategories.name: Candles
product_subcategories.description: Shabbat candle sets, havdalah candles, and candleholders.

product_subcategories.name: Kiddush Cups
product_subcategories.description: Traditional and contemporary kiddush cups in glass, silver, and ceramic.

product_subcategories.name: Books
product_subcategories.description: Prayerbooks, Torah commentaries, and Jewish spirituality titles.

product_subcategories.name: Home Decor
product_subcategories.description: Wall art, blessing prints, and decorative ritual objects for the home.

products.name: Traditional Shabbat Candles (12-Pack)
products.description: Classic white Shabbat candles with a clean, even burn. Perfect for weekly use or to keep on hand for guests.

products.name: Dripless Shabbat Candle Set (24-Pack)
products.description: Long-lasting, dripless Shabbat candles that burn cleanly and are ideal for families who light weekly.

products.name: Hand-Dipped Beeswax Shabbat Candles (Pair)
products.description: Warm, honey-scented beeswax candles hand-dipped by a local artisan, creating a soft golden glow for Shabbat.

products.name: Deluxe Shabbat Candle Set (36-Pack)
products.description: Value pack of dripless Shabbat candles with extended burn time, ideal for regular hosts and large households.

products.name: Braided Havdalah Candle
products.description: Traditional braided Havdalah candle in deep blue and white, with a bright flame to close out Shabbat.

products.name: Simple Glass Kiddush Cup
products.description: A clear, minimalist glass kiddush cup that lets the wine’s color shine. Ideal as a first cup or for guests.

products.name: Silver-Plated Kiddush Cup with Saucer
products.description: Elegant silver-plated kiddush cup with a matching saucer and subtle engraved vine design.

products.name: Hand-Painted Ceramic Kiddush Cup (Blue)
products.description: A vibrant blue ceramic kiddush cup with hand-painted pomegranates and the Hebrew word "Shabbat".

products.name: Colorful Kids’ Kiddush Cup (Acrylic)
products.description: Sturdy acrylic kiddush cup sized for children, with a playful multicolor design.

products.name: Introduction to Kabbalah: A Gentle Guide
products.description: An accessible overview of key Kabbalistic ideas, texts, and practices, written for curious beginners.

products.name: Weekly Torah Portion Companion
products.description: Short essays, reflections, and questions for each parashah, designed for personal study or table discussion.

products.name: Jewish Mindfulness Practices
products.description: A practical handbook of Jewishly-rooted mindfulness meditations, blessings, and daily rituals.

products.name: "Shalom" Hebrew Wall Art Print
products.description: A minimalist black-and-white print featuring the Hebrew word "Shalom" to bring a sense of peace to your home.

products.name: Birkat HaBayit Home Blessing Plaque
products.description: A colorful wall plaque with the traditional Hebrew blessing for the home, accented with pomegranate motifs.

counselors.name: Rabbi Miriam Gold
counselors.title: Senior Spiritual Counselor & Rabbi
counselors.bio: Rabbi Miriam blends traditional Jewish wisdom with trauma‑informed pastoral counseling. She works with individuals navigating life transitions, grief, spiritual questions, and relationship challenges, and is known for her grounded presence and clear, compassionate guidance.

counselors.name: Rabbi David Stein
counselors.title: Pastoral Counselor & Ritual Guide
counselors.bio: Rabbi David offers short‑term and ongoing spiritual counseling for people seeking to reconnect with Jewish practice, process complex emotions, or prepare for major life‑cycle moments. His approach integrates text study, mindfulness, and gentle humor.

counselors.name: Rabbi Yael Levy
counselors.title: Spiritual Director
counselors.bio: Rabbi Yael accompanies seekers who want to deepen prayer, develop a sustainable spiritual practice, or listen for the sacred in everyday life. Sessions often include guided visualization, niggunim (wordless melodies), and tailored ritual.

counselors.name: Rabbi Rina Levinson
counselors.title: Grief & Mourning Specialist
counselors.bio: Rabbi Rina supports mourners before and after shiva, people saying Kaddish, and those living with anticipatory grief. She weaves together gentle listening, traditional mourning practices, and tools for remembering and storytelling.

counselors.name: Rabbi Sarah Bloom
counselors.title: Family & Parenting Counselor
counselors.bio: Rabbi Sarah partners with parents and caregivers to bring more calm, connection, and Jewish wisdom into home life. She offers practical tools for creating rituals with children and navigating family conflict with compassion.

counselors.name: Rabbi Noam Reich
counselors.title: Online Spiritual Counselor
counselors.bio: Rabbi Noam works primarily online with individuals around the world who are seeking a non‑judgmental space to ask big questions, reconnect with Jewish identity, or explore spiritual practice outside a traditional synagogue.

counselors.name: Morah Hannah Green
counselors.title: Mindfulness & Embodied Practice Guide
counselors.bio: Hannah offers spiritually grounded counseling that draws on Jewish mindfulness, gentle somatic awareness, and breathwork. She particularly enjoys working with people who feel "stuck" and want to reconnect with their bodies and inner wisdom.

counselors.name: Rabbi Josh Kaplan
counselors.title: Community Rabbi & Counselor
counselors.bio: Rabbi Josh provides short‑term pastoral counseling around work stress, relationships, spiritual doubts, and community conflict. His style is warm, direct, and practical, with an emphasis on next steps and small rituals for daily life.

counselors.name: Rabbi Levi Mandel
counselors.title: Senior Lifecycle Counselor
counselors.bio: Rabbi Levi accompanies individuals and couples preparing for major milestones—marriage, retirement, becoming grandparents—and those reflecting on legacy, forgiveness, and meaning later in life.

counselors.name: Cantor Eli Mizrahi
counselors.title: Music‑Centered Spiritual Counselor
counselors.bio: Cantor Eli offers spiritually focused sessions that incorporate song, chanting, and guided niggunim for those who find music to be a primary path to the sacred. He also supports people feeling disconnected from prayer.

counselors.name: Maya Aronson, LCSW
counselors.title: Clinical Social Worker & Spiritual Counselor
counselors.bio: Maya integrates clinical training with Jewish spiritual resources, supporting clients struggling with anxiety, boundaries, and the impact of family systems. She is experienced in interfaith and multi‑cultural contexts.

counselors.name: Rabbi Tamar Rosen
counselors.title: Visiting Spiritual Counselor
counselors.bio: Rabbi Tamar primarily offers in‑home and on‑site spiritual counseling sessions connected to ritual life—preparing for holidays, marking anniversaries of loss, and creating ceremonies for new beginnings.

counselor_reviews.title: Grounded and deeply caring
counselor_reviews.body: Rabbi Miriam held space for some of the hardest conversations of my life with so much gentleness.

counselor_reviews.title: Helped me find my own path
counselor_reviews.body: I appreciated that she never pushed a particular kind of observance, just helped me listen for what was true.

counselor_reviews.title: Very supportive
counselor_reviews.body: I felt seen and heard. Occasionally I wanted more concrete homework, but overall it was excellent.

counselor_reviews.title: Exactly what I needed in mourning
counselor_reviews.body: Her guidance around shiva and saying Kaddish made everything feel less overwhelming.

counselor_reviews.title: Wise and practical
counselor_reviews.body: We always ended with one small, doable step that carried me through the week.

counselor_reviews.title: Compassionate listener
counselor_reviews.body: I never felt rushed. She held silence in a way that let me access my own wisdom.

counselor_reviews.title: Helped me reconnect with Shabbat
counselor_reviews.body: We designed simple rituals that actually fit my life as a single parent.

counselor_reviews.title: Deep spiritual insight
counselor_reviews.body: She brought texts I’d never seen before that spoke exactly to what I was wrestling with.

counselor_reviews.title: Affirming for LGBTQ+ folks
counselor_reviews.body: As a queer Jew I felt completely safe and affirmed in our sessions.

counselor_reviews.title: Very helpful over several months
counselor_reviews.body: Over eight sessions we slowly untangled some long-standing family patterns.

counselor_reviews.title: Kind, clear, and honest
counselor_reviews.body: She was willing to ask hard questions but always with so much kindness.

counselor_reviews.title: Online sessions worked great
counselor_reviews.body: The video sessions felt surprisingly personal and grounded even over distance.

counselor_reviews.title: Supported me through burnout
counselor_reviews.body: As a Jewish professional I needed someone who understood the unique pressures of this work.

counselor_reviews.title: Powerful grief support
counselor_reviews.body: She helped me mark my father’s yahrzeit in a way that felt deeply healing.

counselor_reviews.title: Truly top-notch counseling
counselor_reviews.body: I’ve worked with therapists before, but this was the first time my spiritual life was really part of the conversation.

counselor_reviews.title: Helpful around religious trauma
counselor_reviews.body: Rabbi David gave me language and tools to separate harmful messages from the core of Judaism.

counselor_reviews.title: Good mix of text and talk
counselor_reviews.body: We studied short pieces together that opened up new ways of seeing my situation.

counselor_reviews.title: Great premarital conversations
counselor_reviews.body: He helped us talk through important topics we hadn’t realized we were avoiding.

counselor_reviews.title: Perfect for online-only support
counselor_reviews.body: Living abroad, I was grateful to find a rabbi who could meet entirely online and understand my situation.

counselor_reviews.title: Warm and non-judgmental
counselor_reviews.body: I felt very accepted as I explored my relationship to tradition.

counseling_bookings.notes: Follow-up to initial grief support session.
Focus on burnout and sustainable Shabbat practice.
Premarital session focused on finances and family expectations.
Preparing for first yahrzeit; may need to adjust time if family can join.
Session canceled due to client schedule conflict; to be rescheduled later in the month.
Client is abroad; please keep to 50 minutes due to time zone.
